content.problemStatement:
You are given an array AAA of size NNN.

You can perform the following operation as many times as you want (possibly, zero):


Choose integers L,R,XL, R, XL,R,X such that 1‚â§L‚â§R‚â§N1 \le L \le R \le N1‚â§L‚â§R‚â§N and 1‚â§X<2301 \le X < 2^{30}1‚â§X<230, and do any **one** of the following:

For all L‚â§i‚â§RL \le i \le RL‚â§i‚â§R, set AiA_iAi‚Äã to Ai‚äïXA_i \oplus XAi‚Äã‚äïX.

‚äï\oplus‚äï denotes the bitwise XOR operation.
For all L‚â§i‚â§RL \le i \le RL‚â§i‚â§R, set AiA_iAi‚Äã to gcd‚Å°(Ai,X)\gcd(A_i, X)gcd(Ai‚Äã,X).


That is, choose a subarray of AAA and an integer XXX, and either replace every element of the subarray with its GCD with XXX, or replace every element of the subarray with its bitwise XOR with XXX.

Compute the **minimum** number of operations required to make all elements of the array equal to KKK.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of two lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and KKK ‚Äî the size of the array, and the required value.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers ‚Äî A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äã,A2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äã




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line one integer: the minimum number of operations.
constraints: The sum of NNN across all tests does not exceed 2‚ãÖ1052 \cdot 10^52‚ãÖ105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: We can do the following operations:

Choose L=2,R=3,X=2L = 2, R = 3, X = 2L=2,R=3,X=2 and the gcd operation. This turns the array AAA into [2,2,2,1][2, 2, 2, 1][2,2,2,1].
Choose L=4,R=4,X=3L = 4, R = 4, X = 3L=4,R=4,X=3 and the xor operation. This turns the array AAA into [2,2,2,2][2, 2, 2, 2][2,2,2,2].

It can be proven that it is impossible to make all elements equal to 222 in fewer than 222 moves, and so this is optimal.
Test case 333: Choose L=1,R=2,X=2L = 1, R = 2, X = 2L=1,R=2,X=2 and the gcd operation.
